sections.label: Section 470
sections.original: θῶμεν θεοῖσί τʼ ἀκροθίνιʼ ἐξέλῃς ,
sections.translation: and you have chosen out first-fruits for the gods , I wish to march with you against the Argives ’ country and at my coming lay Hellas waste with war , that they in turn may know the taste of ill .

sections.label: Section 474
sections.original: εἰ τοῦ παρόντος τοῦδʼ ἀπαλλαχθεὶς κακοῦ
sections.translation: If I could rid the city of this present curse

sections.label: Section 475
sections.original: πόλιν νεμοίμην ὡς τὸ πρίν ποτʼ ἀσφαλῆ ,
sections.translation: and restore it to its old security , I should indeed feel deep gratitude towards the gods . But , as for sacking Argos and the pasture-lands of Hellas with the spear , it is no such easy task as you say .

sections.label: Section 479
sections.original: οὐ τούσδʼ ἀριστέας φασὶν Ἑλλήνων μολεῖν ;
sections.translation: Do they not say that here came the greatest chiefs of Hellas ?

sections.label: Section 480
sections.original: κοὐ μεμφόμεσθά γʼ , ἀλλʼ ἄδην ἐλαύνομεν .
sections.translation: Yes , and I do not scorn them ; I have enough to do in driving them away .

sections.label: Section 481
sections.original: οὐκ οὖν κτανόντες τούσδε πᾶν εἰργάσμεθα ;
sections.translation: Well , when we slay these , is our task not fully done ?

sections.label: Section 482
sections.original: μή νυν τὰ πόρρω τἀγγύθεν μεθεὶς σκόπει .
sections.translation: Do not leave the present need to look to distant schemes .

sections.label: Section 483
sections.original: ἀρκεῖν ἔοικέ σοι παθεῖν , δρᾶσαι δὲ μή .
sections.translation: You are , it seems , content to suffer and make no return .

sections.label: Section 484
sections.original: πολλῆς γὰρ ἄρχω κἀνθάδʼ ὢν τυραννίδος .
sections.translation: Yes , for I rule a great empire , even though I am here .

sections.label: Section 485
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἴτε λαιὸν εἴτε δεξιὸν κέρας
sections.translation: But on the left wing or the right or in the centre of the allies you may plant your shield and marshal your troops .

sections.label: Section 488
sections.original: μόνος μάχεσθαι πολεμίοις , Ἕκτορ , θέλω .
sections.translation: Alone I will face the foe , Hector . But if you are ashamed , after all your previous toil ,

sections.label: Section 490
sections.original: πρύμνας , πονήσας τὸν πάρος πολὺν χρόνον ,
sections.translation: to have no share in firing their ships ’ prows , place me face to face with Achilles and his army .

sections.label: Section 492
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστʼ ἐκείνῳ θοῦρον ἐντάξαι δόρυ .
sections.translation: Against that man you cannot range your eager spear .

sections.label: Section 493
sections.original: καὶ μὴν λόγος γʼ ἦν ὡς ἔπλευσʼ ἐπʼ Ἴλιον .
sections.translation: Why , it was surely said he sailed to Ilium .

sections.label: Section 494
sections.original: ἔπλευσε καὶ πάρεστιν · ἀλλὰ μηνίων
sections.translation: He sailed and he is here ; but he is angry

sections.label: Section 495
sections.original: στρατηλάταισιν οὐ συναίρεται δόρυ .
sections.translation: and takes no part with the other chieftains in the battle .

sections.label: Section 496
sections.original: τίς δὴ μετʼ αὐτὸν ἄλλος εὐδοξεῖ στρατοῦ ;
sections.translation: Who next to him has won a name in their army ?

sections.label: Section 497
sections.original: Αἴας ἐμοὶ μὲν οὐδὲν ἡσσᾶσθαι δοκεῖ
sections.translation: Aias and the son of Tydeus are , I take it , in no way his inferiors ; there is Odysseus , a wheedling rascal , but bold enough indeed ,

sections.label: Section 500
sections.original: καὶ πλεῖστα χώραν τήνδʼ ἀνὴρ καθυβρίσας ·
sections.translation: and of all men he has wrought most outrage on this country . For he came by night to Athena ’ s shrine and stole her image and took it to the Argive ships ; next he came inside our battlements , clad as a vagrant in a beggar ’ s garb , and loudly did he curse

sections.label: Section 505
sections.original: ἠρᾶτο , πεμφθεὶς Ἰλίου κατάσκοπος ·
sections.translation: the Argives , sent as a spy to Ilium ; and then went out again , when he had slain the sentinels and warders at the gate . He is always to be found lurking in ambush about the altar of Thymbrean Apollo near the city . In him we have a troubling pest to wrestle with .

sections.label: Section 510
sections.original: οὐδεὶς ἀνὴρ εὔψυχος ἀξιοῖ λάθρᾳ
sections.translation: No brave man thinks it right to kill his foe in secret , but to meet him face to face . If I can catch this fellow alive , who , as you say , sits in stealthy ambush and plots his mischief , I will impale him at the outlet of the gates

sections.label: Section 515
sections.original: στήσω πετεινοῖς γυψὶ θοινατήριον .
sections.translation: and set him up for winged vultures to make their meal upon . This is the death he ought to die , pirate and temple-robber that he is .

sections.label: Section 518
sections.original: νῦν μὲν καταυλίσθητε · καὶ γὰρ εὐφρόνη .
sections.translation: To your quarters now , for it is night . For you I will myself point out a spot where your army

sections.label: Section 520
sections.original: τὸν σὸν νυχεῦσαι τοῦ τεταγμένου δίχα .
sections.translation: can watch this night apart from our array . Our password is Phoebus , if perhaps there should be need of it ; hear and remember it , and tell it to the Thracian army . You must advance in front of our ranks and keep a watchful guard , and receive Dolon ,

sections.label: Section 525
sections.original: δέχθαι Δόλωνα · καὶ γάρ , εἴπερ ἐστὶ σῶς ,
sections.translation: who went to spy on the ships , for he , if he is safe , is even now approaching the camp of Troy .

sections.label: Section 1.527
sections.original: — τίνος ἁ φυλακά ; τίς ἀμείβει
sections.translation: Whose watch is it ? who relieves me ? night ’ s earlier stars are on the wane , and the seven

sections.label: Section 1.530
sections.original: Πλειάδες αἰθέριαι · μέσα δʼ αἰετὸς οὐρανοῦ ποτᾶται .
sections.translation: Pleiads mount the sky ; in the middle of the heavens the eagle floats . Rouse yourselves , why delay ? Up from your beds to the watch ! Do you not see the moonlight ?

sections.label: Section 1.535
sections.original: — ἀὼς δὴ πέλας , ἀὼς
sections.translation: Dawn is near , dawn is coming , and lo ! a star that heralds it . Who was told off to the first watch ? The son of Mygdon , whom they call Coroebus .

sections.label: Section 1.540
sections.original: — τίς γὰρ ἐπʼ αὐτῷ ; — Κίλικας Παίων
sections.translation: Who after him ? The Paeonian contingent roused the Cilicians , and the Mysians us . Is it not then high time we went and roused the Lycians for the fifth watch ,

sections.label: Section 1.545
sections.original: καιρὸς κλήρου κατὰ μοῖραν ;
sections.translation: as the lot decided ?

sections.label: Section 1.550
sections.original: γήρυϊ παιδολέτωρ μελοποιὸν ἀηδονὶς μέριμναν .
sections.translation: her piteous plaint , the nightingale that slew her child . Already on Ida they are pasturing the flocks , and over the night I catch the shrill pipe ’ s note . Sleep charms my eyes ,

sections.label: Section 1.555
sections.original: ὕπνος · ἅδιστος γὰρ ἔβα
sections.translation: for sleep is sweetest at dawn to tired eyelids . Why does not our scout draw near , whom Hector sent to spy on the fleet ? He is so long away , I have my fears .

sections.label: Section 1.560
sections.original: — ἀλλʼ ἦ κρυπτὸν λόχον ἐσπαίσας
sections.translation: Is it possible he has plunged into a hidden ambush and been slain ? Perhaps . I am afraid . My counsel is we go and rouse the Lycians for the fifth watch , as the lot ordained . Exit Chorus

sections.label: Section 565
sections.original: Διόμηδες , οὐκ ἤκουσας — ἢ κενὸς ψόφος
sections.translation: Did you not hear , Diomedes , the clash of arms or is it an idle noise that rings in my ears ?

sections.label: Section 567
sections.original: οὔκ , ἀλλὰ δεσμὰ πωλικῶν ἐξ ἀντύγων
sections.translation: No , it is the rattle of steel harness on the chariot rails ; I , too , was afraid , till I perceived it was the clang of horses ’ chains .

sections.label: Section 570
sections.original: ὅρα κατʼ ὄρφνην μὴ φύλαξιν ἐντύχῃς .
sections.translation: Beware lest you stumble upon the guard in the darkness .

sections.label: Section 571
sections.original: φυλάξομαί τοι κἀν σκότῳ τιθεὶς πόδα .
sections.translation: I will take good care how I advance even in the gloom .

sections.label: Section 572
sections.original: ἢν δʼ οὖν ἐγείρῃς , οἶσθα σύνθημα στρατοῦ ;
sections.translation: If however you should rouse them , do you know their password ?

sections.label: Section 573
sections.original: Φοῖβον Δόλωνος οἶδα σύμβολον κλύων .
sections.translation: Yes , it is Phoebus ; I heard Dolon use it .

sections.label: Section 574
sections.original: ἔα ·
sections.translation: Ah ! I see the enemy have left this bivouac .

sections.label: Section 575
sections.original: καὶ μὴν Δόλων γε τάσδʼ ἔφραζεν Ἕκτορος
sections.translation: Yet Dolon surely said that here was Hector ’ s couch , against whom this sword of mine is drawn .

sections.label: Section 577
sections.original: τί δῆτʼ ἂν εἴη ; μῶν λόχος βέβηκέ ποι ;
sections.translation: What can it mean ? Has his company withdrawn elsewhere ?

sections.label: Section 578
sections.original: ἴσως ἐφʼ ἡμῖν μηχανὴν στήσων τινά .
sections.translation: Perhaps to form some stratagem against us .

sections.label: Section 579
sections.original: θρασὺς γὰρ Ἕκτωρ νῦν , ἐπεὶ κρατεῖ , θρασύς .
sections.translation: Yes , for Hector is bold now , by reason of his victory , bold .

sections.label: Section 580
sections.original: τί δῆτʼ , Ὀδυσσεῦ , δρῶμεν ; οὐ γὰρ ηὕρομεν
sections.translation: What then are we to do , Odysseus ? We have not found the man asleep ; our hopes are dashed .

sections.label: Section 582
sections.original: στείχωμεν ὡς τάχιστα ναυστάθμων πέλας .
sections.translation: Let us go to the fleet with what speed we may . Some god , whichever it be that gives him his good luck , is preserving him ; against fate we must not strive .

sections.label: Section 585
sections.original: οὐκ οὖν ἐπʼ Αἰνέαν ἢ τὸν ἔχθιστον Φρυγῶν
sections.translation: Then should we two not go against Aeneas or Paris , most hateful of Phrygians , and with our swords cut off their heads ?

sections.label: Section 587
sections.original: πῶς οὖν ἐν ὄρφνῃ πολεμίων ἀνὰ στρατὸν
sections.translation: Well , how in the darkness can you find them among a hostile army , and slay them without risk ?

sections.label: Section 589
sections.original: αἰσχρόν γε μέντοι ναῦς ἐπʼ Ἀργείων μολεῖν
sections.translation: Yet it would be shameful to go to the Argive ships

sections.label: Section 590
sections.original: δράσαντε μηδὲν πολεμίους νεώτερον .
sections.translation: if we have done the enemy no harm .

sections.label: Section 591
sections.original: πῶς δʼ οὐ δέδρακας ; οὐ κτανόντε ναυστάθμων
sections.translation: What ! no harm ! Have we not slain Dolon who spied upon the anchored fleet , and have we not his spoils safe here ? Or do you expect to sack the entire camp ?

sections.label: Section 594
sections.original: πείθεις , πάλιν στείχωμεν · εὖ δʼ εἴη τυχεῖν .
sections.translation: I agree , let us return ; and good luck go with us !

sections.label: Section 595
sections.original: ποῖ δὴ λιπόντες Τρωικῶν ἐκ τάξεων
sections.translation: Where are you going , away from the Trojan ranks , with sorrow gnawing at your hearts , because the god does not grant you two to slay Hector or Paris ? Have you not heard that Rhesus has come to aid Troy in no mean fashion ?

sections.label: Section 600
sections.original: ὃς εἰ διοίσει νύκτα τήνδʼ ἐς αὔριον ,
sections.translation: If he survives this night until the dawn , neither Achilles nor Aias ’ s spear can stop him from utterly destroying the Argive fleet , razing its palisades and carrying this the onslaught of his lance far and wide within the gates .

sections.label: Section 605
sections.original: τοῦτον κατακτὰς πάντʼ ἔχεις . τὰς δʼ Ἕκτορος
sections.translation: Slay him , and all is yours ; let Hector ’ s sleep alone , no throat-cutting slaughter ; for he shall find death at another hand .

sections.label: Section 608
sections.original: δέσποινʼ Ἀθάνα , φθέγματος γὰρ ᾐσθόμην
sections.translation: Queen Athena , it is the well-known accent of your voice I hear ; for you are always

sections.label: Section 610
sections.original: παροῦσʼ ἀμύνεις τοῖς ἐμοῖς ἀεί ποτε ·
sections.translation: at my side to help me in my toil . Tell us where that man lies asleep ; in what part of the barbarian army is he stationed ?

sections.label: Section 613
sections.original: ὅδʼ ἐγγὺς ἧσται κοὐ συνήθροισται στρατῷ ,
sections.translation: Here lies he close at hand , not marshalled with the other troops , but outside the ranks Hector has given him quarters ,

sections.label: Section 615
sections.original: Ἕκτωρ , ἕως ἂν νὺξ ἀμείψηται φάος .
sections.translation: till night gives place to day . And near him his white horses are tethered to his Thracian chariot , easy to see in the darkness ; they shine like the plumage of a river swan . Slay their master and bear them off

sections.label: Section 620
sections.original: κάλλιστον οἴκοις σκῦλον · οὐ γὰρ ἔσθʼ ὅπου
sections.translation: home , glorious spoils ; for nowhere else in all the world is such a team to be found .
